annotations.decision: no past medical history who presents with sudden onset of severe headache

The patient was transferred to [**Hospital1 69**] for further management.
The patient was taken to arteriogram on admission which showed a rupture of the right ICA aneurysm.
Nimodipine, Dilantin and Nipride
sedated
intubated

Cardiovascular, sinus tachycardia.
Chest clear to auscultation.
Abdomen benign.
Extremities, no edema.

Post procedure the patient was awake, alert and oriented times three with mild left drift.  EOMs were full. No facial droop, moving all extremities strongly.
IV fluids were going at 125/hour.
The patient had vent drain in place
The patient was maintained in the Surgical Intensive Care Unit from [**2170-8-30**] until [**2170-9-18**] and monitored for vasospasm.
The patient was maintained on IV fluids at 125/hour
with IV albumin and IV neo
to keep systolic blood pressure 160-190.
The patient had vent drain weaned and discontinued
vent drain was discontinued
the patient's neo drip was discontinued
The patient had episode of vasospasm which manifested as severe left facial droop, severe left hemiparesis
The patient was placed back on neo,
her IV fluids were increased to 250/hour
and she was restarted on albumin.
These symptoms resolved.
On [**2170-9-14**] the patient's O2 sats declined to 85% on room air.
The patient was sent for spiral CT to rule out PE which was ruled out.
She was also sent for bilateral lower extremity dopplers of her lower extremity which were negative for DVT.
The patient had pulmonary toilet and chest PT and O2 sats came back to their normal range, 99%.
The patient's neurologic status after resolving hemiparesis remained stable.
The patient was weaned off IV fluid and IV neo
patient was transferred to the regular floor
